description:
## Background

Users need a stable, self-contained way to copy an Issue Location. Issue IDs can collide across Workspaces, so the canonical external link must include the Workspace path. The accepted scheme is `beadsmithy:///absolute/workspace/path/issue/<issue-id>`.

Triage: `ready-for-agent`.

## What to build

Define the canonical Issue Location URI contract and add a Copy deep link action to resolved Issue Detail. Copying produces a canonical `beadsmithy` URI and gives local checkmark/tooltip feedback without a global notification.

## Technical Approach

- Add a pure Issue Location URI module with typed generation and parsing results.
- Generate a three-slash custom URI whose path contains the absolute Workspace path followed by the terminal `/issue/<issue-id>` suffix.
- Percent-encode and decode path segments correctly, including spaces and URI-reserved characters. Parse the final suffix without being confused by a Workspace directory named `issue`.
- Validate scheme, absolute-path shape, final suffix, non-empty Issue ID, percent encoding, and unsupported extra path components. Keep malformed inputs as typed failures for later banner presentation.
- Cover Unix paths as required. Include Windows drive-path behavior only if it remains trivial and does not complicate macOS/Linux delivery.
- Add an icon-only Copy deep link button in the top-right of resolved Issue Detail.
- Copy the generated URI through the supported clipboard boundary.
- On success, temporarily show a checkmark and a `Copied!` tooltip with accessible feedback. Do not show a global banner.
- Omit the action from no-selection and Issue-not-found detail states.

## Considerations

- Absolute filesystem paths are intentionally exposed by this local-machine-specific link format.
- The generator receives the canonical Current Workspace path already published with the Issue Explorer snapshot.
- Clipboard failure should remain local to the control and must not falsely display success.
- Parsing is included now so outgoing and incoming links share one contract; OS delivery is implemented later.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Resolved Issue Detail exposes a top-right Copy deep link button.
- [ ] Copied URIs use the canonical `beadsmithy:///.../issue/<issue-id>` format with correct encoding.
- [ ] Copy success temporarily shows a checkmark and `Copied!` tooltip without global feedback.
- [ ] Clipboard failure does not show a false success state.
- [ ] Copy is absent from no-selection and Issue-not-found states.
- [ ] Parser and generator round-trip valid Unix Issue Locations.
- [ ] Malformed schemes, paths, encodings, suffixes, and Issue IDs return typed failures.
- [ ] Workspace directory names containing `issue` do not break suffix parsing.

## Test Plan

- Add exhaustive pure parser/generator tests for Unix roots, spaces, `#`, `?`, percent characters, Unicode, nested `issue` directory names, malformed percent sequences, empty IDs, missing suffixes, and extra components.
- Add Issue Detail interaction tests with a mocked clipboard for success, failure, timeout/reset behavior, accessible tooltip feedback, and absent states.
- Run renderer tests, TypeScript build, and Ultracite.
title: Copy canonical Issue Location deep links
